---
title: "Project proposal"
author: "Team name"
output: html_document
---

```{r load-packages, message = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
```

## 1. Data

The data set contains sub-hourly sap flow measurements recorded from 3–4 tree species at a forested field site. Each observation includes a time stamp, a species identifier, and an inner and outer sap flow rate. Before analysis, sub-hourly readings will be averaged to daily means to reduce variation from high frequency observations, while preserving seasonal trends. Any missing values will be identified and removed prior to modeling.

## 2. Exploratory Visualization

Three visualizations are planned. First, violin plots/boxplots of daily mean sap flow grouped by species will reveal whether water transport rates differ across species. Second, a time series of sap flow for each species across the growing season will highlight chronological differences and whether species diverge in water use at particular times of year. Third, a plot of sap flow variability by species will help identify if some species are more consistent in their water transport than others, a pattern that can be just as informative as differences in mean rates.

## 3. Possible question and method

Do tree species differ significantly in their sap flow rates across either the growing season, or just in general?

average the sub-hourly observations into daily averages and then compare to other trees. Will need to compare baseline rates as well as rates durring different seasons. Can also use the plots to help visualize and indentify variablility.
